reasoning: Kaylee McKeown holds the women's 50m backstroke long course world record at 26.86, set in October 2023. Katharine Berkoff set a new American record of 26.97 in June 2025 (becoming #2 all-time), and won the 2025 World Championships in July/August, but fell short of McKeown's record. With the 2025 World Championships already over, the period from December 2025 to March 2026 generally lacks major international peak competitions (some Grand Prix / Pro Swim Series and state championships will occur, but athletes rarely fully taper for them). It is therefore highly unlikely that the record will be broken in the next 1-3 months. If McKeown breaks her own record, she would still be the record holder. Thus, the only risk is someone else (like Berkoff or Regan Smith) swimming faster than 26.86. The probability remains very high, decreasing slightly as the temporal horizon expands to March 2026.
